diagrams.title: Linear Independence in R² - Independent Case
diagrams.description: Two vectors (1,0) and (0,1) pointing along x and y axes, forming a right angle. They span entire 2D plane.
diagrams.purpose: Visualize that non-parallel vectors in R² are independent and span the plane

diagrams.title: Linear Dependence in R² - Dependent Case
diagrams.description: Two vectors (1,2) and (2,4) lying along the same line. They point in exactly the same or opposite directions.
diagrams.purpose: Show that collinear vectors (scalar multiples) are always linearly dependent

diagrams.title: Linear Independence in R³ - Triangular Pyramid
diagrams.description: Three vectors not lying in same plane, forming edges of a triangular pyramid from origin.
elements: Dashed lines forming triangle at tips showing they don't lie in same plane
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrate that 3 vectors in R³ are independent only when not coplanar

diagrams.title: Row Echelon Form - Independent vs Dependent
diagrams.description: Matrix transformation showing pivot positions. Left: 3 pivots (independent). Right: one zero row (dependent).
elements: Matrix with leading 1s in different columns
Zero row at bottom for dependent case
diagrams.purpose: Visualize how to determine independence using row echelon form

diagrams.title: Determinant as Area/Volume Test
diagrams.description: Parallelogram (R²) or parallelepiped (R³) with vectors as edges. Zero area/volume means dependent.
diagrams.purpose: Understand determinant geometrically as test for independence
